poster_title: Teaching Language Models to Critique via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zhihui Xie, Jie Chen, Liyu Chen, Weichao Mao, Jingjing Xu, Lingpeng Kong
paper_info: Proceedings of the 42nd International Conference on Machine Learning, Vancouver, Canada, 2025

content: Recent LLM advancements enable self-improvement through iterative feedback.
Self-critique methods often lack actionable guidance and can degrade performance.
Need for a specialized critic model to provide effective feedback.
Feedback must accurately assess solution correctness.
Provide informative and actionable suggestions for improvement.
Automated tools and reward models often fail to meet these criteria.
Propose CTRL: decouples critic from task-performing model.
Two-stage training: supervised finetuning with execution feedback, followed by GRPO.
Critic generates feedback to maximize correction performance.
Evaluated on CodeContests, LiveCodeBench, MBPP+, and JudgeBench.
Tested with both base and stronger generator models.
Demonstrated effectiveness across different problem domains and model scales.
panels.figure_caption: Majority voting accuracy improvement and value network training curve

content: Significant enhancement in pass rates and error mitigation.
Up to 106.1% relative improvement on CodeContests.
Generalization of critic LLMs across various benchmarks.
CTRL offers a promising approach for training specialized critic models.
Demonstrates significant improvements and generalization in code generation.
Future work can explore broader applications and further optimizations.

Fig1.caption: Figure 10. (a) Majority voting accuracy improvement with increasing votes. (b) Training curve of the value network.
Fig1.description: The figure consists of two parts: (a) a chart showing the improvement in majority voting accuracy as the number of votes increases; (b) the training curve of the value network.
